title: Morocco forced into two injury changes ahead of World Cup opener with Brazil | Flashscore.com
original_text:
Morocco have been hit by the loss of two starters to injury, ‚ÅÝwith Nayef Aguerd and Abde Ezzalzouli replaced in their World Cup squad.

Saudi-based defender Marwane Saadane and striker ‚ÄåAmine Sbai have been added as replacements, the Moroccan federation and FIFA ‚Äåconfirmed.

Aguerd , 30, has not played since the start ‚Äåof March because of a groin injury, which required surgery. ‚ÄåHis recovery stalled in April when it was discovered ‚Äåthat he had a fracture of his pubic bone.

Morocco coach Mohamed Ouahabi kept up hope Aguerd would recover in time, but ‚Äådecided on Thursday that he would not ‚ÅÝbe ready for this ‚Äåmonth‚Äôs tournament in Canada, Mexico and the United States.

The 24-year-old Ezzalzouli ‚Äãwas hurt in a freak incident in the weekend‚Äôs friendly against Norway in Harrison, New Jersey.

As ‚ÄãMorocco defended a corner kick, teammate Chadi Riad landed awkwardly on Ezzalzouli's right knee. Ezzalzouli attempted to continue ‚ÅÝplaying, but soon had ‚Äãto be taken off.

Both Aguerd and Ezzalzouli were in the Morocco squad that reached the semi-final of the last World Cup in Qatar, and also the final of the ‚ÄåAfrica Cup of Nations in Morocco in January.

Aguerd, however, was also injured at the last World Cup in the last-16 tie against Spain and missed their three subsequent games at the tournament.

Saadane, 34, debuted for Morocco in 2015 but has been an infrequent squad member since, while 25-year-old Sbai, who plays primarily on the left wing, won a first cap earlier this month in ‚Äåa World Cup warm-up friendly against Burundi .

Both Saadane and ‚ÄãSbai were taken to the US as cover and ‚Äåhave been training with the squad. Saadane featured in Sunday‚Äôs 1-1 draw with Norway as a second half replacement while Sbai was listed among the substitutes.

Morocco open their Group C campaign at the World Cup ‚ÅÝagainst Brazil at the ‚ÅÝNew York/New Jersey Stadium ‚Äåon Saturday.

The 2026 World Cup will be held from June 11th to July 19th in the United States, Canada and Mexico. The tournament will feature 48 national teams and will be played in 16 modern stadiums.
